title: fix(docs): add information about relative paths
body:
Hello, I have found out that relative paths do not work as expected, for example:

```
cat > ~/.config/containers/systemd/test.container <<EOF
[Container]
ContainerName=test
EnvironmentFile=%h/.config/containers/systemd/test.env
Image=quay.io/test/test:latest
EOF
```

A unit will be generated in `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/systemd/generator` therefore the relative path will not work as `test.env` is not copied there.

For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
As @Luap99 wrote, the example you provided here should just work. If it doesn't we need to understand why. Does the example you provided in the first comment not work?

Apologies for the irrelevant example, I copy-pasted a wrong block.

Let me explain my problem: when I use a relative path in a quadlet:

```
EnvironmentFile=test.env
```

it does not work. Shall I go with the following note then:

_Paths are relative to `/` directory and not to the unit file._

Thanks for clarifying. I've just tested it and it works for me:

```
$ cat ~/.config/containers/systemd/test.container
[Container]
Image=quay.io/test/test:latest
EnvironmentFile=test.env

$ /usr/libexec/podman/quadlet -dryrun -user
---test.service---
[X-Container]
Image=quay.io/test/test:latest
EnvironmentFile=test.env

[Unit]
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
SourcePath=/home/yblum.linux/.config/containers/systemd/test.container
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d --env-file /home/yblum.linux/.config/containers/systemd/test.env quay.io/test/test:latest
```

In `ExecStart` you can see: `--env-file /home/yblum.linux/.config/containers/systemd/test.env`

Could is be that you are using an old version? For me:
```
$ /usr/libexec/podman/quadlet --version
5.2.2
```

Hmmm I swear it did not work the other day. I have the same version and it works now :-)

Apologies for the noise, closing.
